title: Investing Basics — Compounding & Owning Companies
learningObjectives: Understand how compound interest creates wealth exponentially
See how buying stock means owning a piece of a company
Recognize that time in market beats timing the market
keyPrinciples: Compound interest: earning returns on your returns
Every stock is a fractional ownership of a real business
Start early — time is the most powerful investing variable
storyPrompts: Einstein (maybe) called it the eighth wonder of the world. {name} calls it the math that changed their mind. $100 invested at 10%, left alone for 30 years: $1,745. No extra deposits. Just time and compounding. 'I should have started yesterday,' {name} thinks. 'But the next best time is now.'

When {name} buys a share of fictional TechCo Inc., they don't just own a piece of paper. They own a slice of every product TechCo sells, every idea their engineers create. 'I'm a part-owner,' {name} says. It sounds crazy. Then it doesn't.

The chart shows {name}'s fictional investment growing: Year 1, modest. Year 5, interesting. Year 10, exciting. Year 20, life-changing. Same amount invested at year 1. Just time doing its silent, powerful work.

decisionCards.scenario: You have {amount} and 40 years before retirement. What do you do?
decisionCards.context: $100 at 10% for 40 years = $4,525. Time is the variable.

choices.label: Save it in a low-yield account
choices.opportunityCost: $50 in savings = $50. $50 invested = $2,262 in 40 years at 10%.

choices.label: Invest it in diversified fictional assets

decisionCards.scenario: The market dropped 20% today. Your Ownership Orchard is down.
decisionCards.context: Market drops are temporary. Panic selling is permanent.

choices.label: Sell everything — protect what's left!
choices.description: Panic selling locks in the loss.
choices.opportunityCost: Market drops recover. Selling at the bottom means you miss the recovery.

choices.label: Buy more — it's on sale!
choices.description: Drops are discounts if you're a long-term holder.

decisionCards.scenario: A hot tip: 'This fictional coin will 10x in a week!'
decisionCards.context: Guaranteed return is the language of scams.

choices.label: Invest your savings in it!
choices.description: Get rich quick is always tempting.

choices.label: Invest a tiny amount you can afford to lose
Pass — guaranteed returns don't exist
choices.description: If it sounds too good, it is.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: Market crash! Everything is red. Your portfolio is down 30%.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: The ultimate investor test. Panic or principle?

choices.label: Sell everything to stop the bleeding
choices.description: Panic selling: the investor's Achilles heel.

choices.label: Hold and rebalance calmly
choices.description: The investor who holds through crashes wins.

choices.label: Buy more at the discounted price
choices.description: Crashes are sales for long-term investors.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: You discover compounding math. $10/week from age 15 = over $1M by retirement.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: This is real. What do you do with this information?

choices.label: Interesting but I'll start when I earn more
choices.description: The most expensive sentence in personal finance.

choices.label: Start $10/week into Ownership Orchard now
choices.description: The math only works if you start.

choices.label: Invest $25/week and tell every friend
choices.description: Evangelize the math — change your circle.

bossChallengeTemplates.scenario: BOSS: You're offered a 'safe' 3% annual return vs a 'risky' 10% return.
bossChallengeTemplates.context: Over 30 years, the difference is life-changing.

choices.label: Mix: 70% growth, 30% safe
choices.description: Balanced risk. Smart at any age.

forecasts: Your Ownership Orchard is blooming! Market growth events increase next week.
You sold during a dip. Market recovery events will test your patience next week.
Consistent investment behavior! Compounding bonus triggered for final session.
You're on the Owner's Path. One final boss stands between you and financial mastery.
